questions.question: Identify the error: 'He don't know the answer.'
questions.explanation: Should be 'doesn't' - he/she/it takes 'doesn't' (third person singular).

questions.question: Correct: 'She is more prettier than her sister.'
options: She is prettier than her sister
She is most pretty than her sister
She is more pretty than her sister
She is very prettier than her sister
questions.correctAnswer: She is prettier than her sister
questions.explanation: 'More prettier' is incorrect. 'Prettier' already is comparative form.

questions.question: Find the error: 'Neither of the students were present.'
Correct the sentence: 'I have went to the store.'
options: I have gone to the store
I have go to the store
I has went to the store
I had go to the store
questions.correctAnswer: I have gone to the store

questions.question: Find error: 'The committee have decided to postpone the meeting.'
questions.explanation: 'Committee' is collective noun, treated as singular: 'has decided'.

questions.question: Correct: 'He is one of the student who works hard.'
options: He is one of the students who works hard
He is one of the students who work hard
He is one of student who work hard
He is one of the students who are working hard
questions.correctAnswer: He is one of the students who work hard

questions.question: Find the error: 'He wrote a letter to his cousin, but they never replied.'
questions.explanation: Referring to one cousin, should use 'he/she', not 'they'.
